channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Asia Rallies on Oil Relief, AI Stocks Face Inflation Threat
item.description: Asian markets advanced on hopes of easing oil volatility and strong tech performance, driven by Nvidia's AI projections. Yet, persistent geopolitical risks and rising energy costs signal inflation threats and central bank caution.

item.title: UAE Gas Field Attack Halts Operations, Oil Prices Top $100
item.description: Operations at the UAE's Shah gas field are stopped after a drone attack, with damage being assessed. The incident heightens regional energy security worries, pushing oil prices up. Brent crude is now trading over $100 per barrel, showing the ongoing threat to Middle East energy infrastructure and its impact on global markets.

item.title: Trent, DMart Valuations Face Pressure as Quick Commerce Booms
item.description: India's leading retailers Trent and DMart are seeing their stock prices near 52-week lows, as investors worry about their high valuations. Fast-expanding quick commerce services, like those from Meesho, are changing how people shop with speed and convenience, directly challenging the established stores. Even with strong consumer confidence in India, these online rivals' speed and pricing methods are creating pressure for Trent and DMart's future growth and profits.

item.title: Mukul Agrawal Re-enters Allcargo, Sirca Paints Stocks for FY27 Comeback
item.description: Mukul Agrawal has reinvested in Allcargo Logistics and Sirca Paints India, two small-cap stocks he had previously divested. Both companies have experienced significant share price depreciation in recent months. Agrawal's move signals a contrarian bet on their FY27 performance, focusing on operational restructuring and strategic expansion over immediate financial metrics.

item.title: Gravita India Buys Copper Business for ₹559 Cr, Valuation in Focus
item.description: Gravita India is expanding its circular metals business by acquiring Rashtriya Metal Industries for ₹559 crore, marking its entry into the copper sector. This move supports sustainability goals and enhances recycling but comes as the company trades at a high valuation. Investors must balance strong growth forecasts and new products against worries about earnings quality and industry risks.

item.title: India Inc governance confusion over who's accountable
item.description: India's corporate governance is becoming unclear, with responsibility for oversight spread thin across boards, committees, and executives. The term 'Those Charged With Governance' (TCWG) adds to this confusion about who is ultimately accountable. As different rules use different terms, companies and auditors find it hard to know who is in charge, risking weaker governance and less investor trust.

item.title: Velmenni Secures ₹30 Crore for Optical Wireless Tech
item.description: Deep-tech firm Velmenni has secured ₹30 crore in Pre-Series A funding, led by pi Ventures, to commercialize its Free Space Optics (FSO) and Light Fidelity (Li-Fi) technology. This light-based wireless communication aims to provide 10Gbps+ backhaul and last-mile connectivity, offering faster deployment and avoiding the costs of fiber trenching and spectrum licensing. The funding will fuel expansion into service provider, defense, and enterprise markets, building on over 50 existing deployments.

item.title: Fertilizer Price Squeeze Looms: Energy Volatility Hits Ag Giants
item.description: Global fertilizer makers face big financial risks because food systems rely heavily on energy. While prices might stabilize briefly, ongoing natural gas price swings, concentrated trade, and new rules mean lower profits and possible supply problems. Companies slow to adopt greener farming methods or with high costs face challenges, while eco-friendly farming grows.

item.title: JLR Pushes EV Strategy Amid Fierce Competition and Big Losses
item.description: Jaguar Land Rover CEO PB Balaji is sticking to the company's strategy after a major cyberattack caused financial problems. Despite falling revenues and big losses, JLR is pressing ahead with its plans for electric vehicles and luxury positioning. But the car industry faces challenges like fierce EV competition and supply chain issues, making JLR's aggressive move a risky bet. Parent company Tata Motors is also struggling, with losses and debt weighing on its recovery.

item.title: CleanMax Buys Kintech Solarbikaner for ₹38 Cr to Boost Gujarat Capacity
item.description: CleanMax Enviro Energy Solutions Limited has agreed to acquire Kintech Solarbikaner Private Limited for ₹38.06 crore. This acquisition is set to boost CleanMax's wind-solar hybrid renewable energy capacity in Gujarat, marking a key step in its growth strategy within the renewable energy sector.

item.title: Camlin Fine Sciences Delays Vinpai Tender Offer, Keeps Unit Listed
item.description: Camlin Fine Sciences (CFSL) has pushed its mandatory tender offer filing for Vinpai shares to early May 2026, delaying from February. The company will not proceed with a squeeze-out, opting to keep Vinpai listed on Euronext Growth Paris to support its European operations, financing, and debt restructuring. CFSL currently holds over 83% of Vinpai's share capital post-convertible bond conversion.
